title: wiborgia leptoptera subsp. cedarbergensis
intro: Wiborgia leptoptera subsp. cedarbergensis is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as the Western Cape broom-wort. It is a perennial herb found in the Western Cape province of South Africa, known for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as colds, sore throats, and skin irritations. Wiborgia leptoptera subsp. cedarbergensis can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in traditional medicine to promote digestion and reduce fever.
